chunk_summary: Guidelines for growth velocity monitoring intervals using WHO/IAP charts, emphasizing age-based schedules, documentation, and high-risk group follow-up.
learning_objectives: Understand age-based growth monitoring intervals for children using WHO/IAP charts.
Learn documentation practices for growth measurements, including weight, height, and BMI.
Identify high-risk groups requiring tighter follow-up in growth monitoring.
Recognize the importance of complete referral packets with growth charts for specialist assessments.
key_facts: Monthly monitoring is recommended for children under 3 years in community settings (ICDS).
Clinical schedule: monthly from 6–12 months, every 2–3 months from 1–2 years, every 3–6 months from 2–5 years, and annually thereafter.
Head circumference should be recorded routinely through 24 months, longer if neurologic concerns exist.
BMI should be computed and plotted annually from 5 years onward to detect early adiposity rebound and obesity.
High-risk groups include preterm infants, low birth weight children, and those recovering from severe acute malnutrition.
reference_quote: A pragmatic clinical schedule is: at every contact in the first 6 months, at least monthly from 6–12 months, every 2–3 months from 1–2 years, every 3–6 months from 2–5 years, and at least annually thereafter.
